name: Pear-Plum Spread
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and core the pears; chop coarsely.
2. Place in a large saucepan and sprinkle with ascorbic acid color keeper.
3. Remove pits from plums and cut each plum into 8 pieces.
4. Stir plums into pears.
5. Wrap plum pits, cloves, and ginger (if desired) in a piece of cheesecloth and place under some of the fruit.
6. Add cinnamon stick and water to the saucepan.
7. Cover and bring to a boil. Simmer 15 to 20 minutes or until fruit is tender.
8. Discard spice bag and cinnamon stick.
9. Puree the fruit mixture in a blender or food processor, or press through a sieve.
10. Return to the saucepan. Simmer, uncovered, 15 to 20 minutes or until mixture coats a metal spoon.
11. Remove from heat. Stir in sweetener.
12. Ladle into hot clean jars, leaving 1/2 inch headspace. Wipe jar rims, seal.
13. Process for 10 minutes in a boiling water bath. Store in a cool, dark, dry place.
14. Processing may be omitted if spreads are to be used within a short period of time. Store in the fridge up to 1 month or freezer up to 3 months.
Makes about 3 1/4 cups, each serving 1 tablespoon (3 grams carbohydrate, 12 calories).
